text: Air rescue marker: SOS pattern 3m tall, bright vs background. Clear area of shadows. Seen from 6km.
SOS signal: 3m tall, bright vs background. Clear vegetation. Range 5km.
Common Purslane (Portulaca oleracea). Succulent leaves, reddish stems. Edible. High omega-3. Slightly sour.
Purslane. Succulent leaves edible. High omega-3.
Take when instructed by authorities or when radioiodine exposure likely. Single dose 130mg adults, 65mg children. Effective if taken 6 hours before to 24 hours after intake.

KI timing:
1. 130mg adult single dose
2. 65mg for children 3-18
3. Take 6h before to 24h after
4. Authorities will instruct
CPU: 45%, RAM: 39%, Temp: 45°C, Battery: 55%, LoRa RSSI: -92dBm, SNR: 3dB
CPU 45°C, Bat 55%, Mesh -92dBm SNR 3dB.
No. Freezing does not kill most pathogens; it merely suspends them. Bacteria and viruses can survive frozen for long periods and become active when thawed. Always treat after thawing.

Freezing fails:
1. Suspends only, does not kill
2. Pathogens survive freeze
3. Reactivate when thawed
4. Treat after melting
Biology survives ice
CPU: 31%, RAM: 27%, Temp: 46°C, Battery: 52%, LoRa RSSI: -99dBm, SNR: 0dB
CPU 46°C, Bat 52%, RSSI -99dBm marginal.
SOS sent. Search team moving to your last known grid.
SOS sent. Search team en route.
Lower half of sternum, center of chest. Heel of one hand, other hand on top. Push 5-6cm deep at 100-120 bpm.

Placement:
1. Center of chest
2. Lower sternum
3. Heel of hand
4. Other hand top
5. Push 5-6cm

Isolated objects act as lightning rods. Electricity seeks highest point. A solitary tree concentrates ground charge and attracts strikes. Side flash and ground current radiate 15-20 meters. Stay clear of lone trees, poles, rocks.

Avoid lone trees:
1. They act as lightning rods
2. Ground current spreads 20m
3. Side flash risk
4. Seek lower ground instead

Arterial slash wound management: Immediate digital pressure on brachial artery. Direct pressure on wound with hemostatic dressing. Elevate arm vertically. If unsuccessful, tourniquet application mandatory on upper arm proximal to slash. Do not delay tourniquet for arterial wounds.

Treat arterial slash:
1. Compress brachial artery and wound simultaneously
2. Elevate arm vertically overhead
3. Mandatory tourniquet on upper arm proximal to slash
Never shelter under single tree. It is a lightning rod. Ground current radiates 20 meters. Stay 20+ meters away. Better to be in open meadow at lowest point than near lone tree. Squat in open.

Never under lone tree:
1. It is a lightning rod
2. Current radiates 20m
3. Better in open low point
4. Squat away from it

Stop the Bleed forearm protocol: 1) Direct pressure with both hands on dressing. 2) Pack wound with hemostatic gauze or cloth. 3) Tourniquet application 2-3 inches above injury on upper arm if needed. 4) Secure tourniquet. 5) Keep patient warm and monitor.

Execute stop the bleed:
1. Apply two-hand pressure with dressing
2. Pack wound with hemostatic gauze or cloth
3. Tourniquet upper arm 2-3 inches above injury if needed
Form: Arms straight, shoulders over hands, push 5-6cm deep, 100-120 bpm, full recoil. 30 compressions then 2 breaths.

Form check:
1. Arms straight
2. Shoulders over hands
3. Push 5-6cm
4. 100-120 bpm
5. Full recoil
Build platform of green logs to insulate from snow. Use birch bark or feather sticks from dry inner wood. Battery and wool works cold.
Insulate from ground. 1. Build log platform. 2. Use inner dry wood for tinder. 3. Battery method works regardless of snow.
5V3A insufficient for peak loads. RPi5 requires 5A transient capability. Voltage drops to 4.5V under load. Fix: 27W PD supply, short 18AWG cable, disable overclock.
5V3A insufficient for peaks. Need: 1. 27W PD supply 2. 18AWG cable <0.5m 3. Reduce peripherals
NODE-B intermittent. Fading in/out.
Node B intermittent. Fading.
Visibility: Triple-press. Check 868.9 MHz, SHORT_FAST preset. Ensure no physical obstruction between units.
Triple-press. Check 868.9 MHz and SHORT_FAST. Clear physical obstructions.
Fast treatment: Immediately lay flat, elevate legs 45 degrees, wrap in blanket, check pulse and breathing, reassure.

Fast treat:
1. Lay flat
2. Legs 45deg
3. Wrap warm
4. Check pulse
If exhausted: Call for help. Use hands-only CPR if unable to give breaths. Push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. Any CPR better than none.

If exhausted:
1. Switch with helper
2. Hands-only OK
3. Push 5-6cm
4. 100-120 bpm
5. Any CPR helps

Brachial artery injury (upper arm): This is a major vessel. Immediate tourniquet application is often required. Place tourniquet on upper arm between shoulder and elbow, 2-3 inches proximal to wound. Compress artery against humerus bone as temporary measure. Elevate limb.

Control brachial artery bleed:
1. Apply tourniquet on upper arm between shoulder and elbow
2. Compress artery against humerus bone temporarily
3. Elevate limb and prepare for rapid evacuation

Analog Watch Method: Point hour hand at the sun. Bisect the angle between the hour hand and 12 o'clock to find South (Northern Hemisphere). For Southern Hemisphere, the same bisection points North. Adjust for Daylight Saving Time by using 1 o'clock instead of 12.
Analog watch: Point hour hand at sun. Halfway between hand and 12 = South (N hem). Reverse for S hem. Adjust 1 hour for DST.
Grass under snow can be dry. Check under evergreen branches. Inside hollow logs. Grass on south facing slopes. Reeds near water. Shake off moisture.

Check evergreens:
1. Under thick branches
2. South facing slopes
3. Inside hollow logs
4. Shake moisture off
Smoke materials: Green leaves = white column. Petroleum = black. Code: 3 puffs = help.
Smoke meaning: 3 puffs = emergency. White = green wood. Black = oil. 6km.
CPU: 54%, RAM: 46%, Temp: 54°C, Battery: 68%, LoRa RSSI: -82dBm, SNR: 5.5dB
CPU 54°C, Bat 68%, LoRa -82dBm SNR 5dB good.
CPU: 74%, RAM: 37%, Temp: 74°C, Battery: 26%, LoRa RSSI: -103dBm, SNR: -3dB
CPU 74°C, RAM 37%, Bat 26%, RSSI -103dBm.
To include Scout-7: Match region EU_868. Set identical channel settings including PSK. Use same modem preset. Ensure frequency slot matches. Reboot Scout-7 and verify in node list.

Add Scout-7:
1. Set EU_868 region
2. Match channel PSK
3. Use same modem preset
4. Match frequency slot
5. Reboot and check
Cut foil wrapper to create thin bridge. Touch ends to battery terminals. Thin section heats and ignites. Works with AA or 9V.
Yes, bridge the terminals. 1. Cut thin strip from wrapper. 2. Touch ends to battery poles. 3. Ignites in seconds, add to tinder.
Finest grade 0000 works best. Unravel to increase oxygen contact. Touch 9V battery terminals. Glows red hot. Blow to flame. Store dry in waterproof container.

Use 0000 grade:
1. Unravel fibers
2. 9V terminal touch
3. Blow to flame
4. Keep dry stored
Use burdock, banana, or skunk cabbage leaves as temporary vessels. Place hot stones inside to boil water. Seal with extra leaves. Fragile.
Large leaves (burdock/banana) hold water. Add hot stones. Seal with extra leaves.

RUL calculated from current capacity vs rated. Good cells maintain <50mOhm ACIR. At 80% capacity remaining, expect 100-200 cycles left depending on depth of discharge. Retire when voltage drops below 3.0V under moderate load.
80% cap = 100-200 cycles left. Retire at 3.0V sag.

Descend quickly but safely. Running increases fall risk. Lightning is faster than you. Prioritize controlled descent to lower elevation. Do not run across open fields. Seek forest or depression once lower.

Descend carefully:
1. Move quickly but safe
2. Lightning is faster
3. Avoid running in open
4. Seek forest when low
Address 0x76 is default for BME280/BMP280 when SDO tied to GND. If SDO tied to VDD, address is 0x77. Check I2C enabled. Check pull-ups. Verify chip powered.

Address or wiring fault.
1. Try address 0x77 if SDO high
2. Enable I2C interface
3. Add pull-up resistors
4. Verify chip power LED

Results: PoE HAT generates 5V from 48V. If switch delivers 15.4W (802.3af) minus losses, only 12W available. Pi 5 needs 15W peak. Must use 802.3at (30W) PoE+ switch or HAT. Also check HAT-to-Pi connector resistance.

PoE power budget insufficient:
1. Upgrade to 802.3at PoE+ (30W) switch
2. Verify PoE HAT supports 3A+ output (official Pi PoE+ HAT)
3. Check 40-pin header connection resistance
Split stick to dry interior. Carve curls attached to stick. Increases surface area. Catches spark easily. Use with ferro rod. Essential wet weather technique.

Carve curls attached:
1. Split dry interior
2. Thin attached shavings
3. High surface area
4. Catches sparks
Place snow in clear bag in sun. Slow melt. Still requires disinfection via UV pen, tablets, or boiling. Eating snow lowers core temp dangerously.
Sun melt in bag. Still need disinfection. Never eat snow direct.
Heat stones in fire, drop into water vessel. Brings to boil. Use non-porous stones only to avoid steam explosion. Replace as they cool.
Heat stones in fire, drop in vessel. Non-porous only. Repeat until rolling boil.
Fix procedure: Triple-click. Verify 868.2 MHz, SHORT_FAST preset. Check antenna orientation vertical.
Triple-click to fix. Verify 868.2 MHz and SHORT_FAST. Keep antenna vertical.
Pairing requires matching region (EU_868), identical channel settings with shared PSK, and same modem preset. Ensure both nodes use same frequency slot and encryption key. Reboot after config.

Match channel settings:
1. Set Region to EU_868
2. Configure identical primary channel
3. Use same PSK encryption key
4. Select Long_Fast modem preset
5. Reboot and wait 60s

Spurting arm bleed (arterial) actions: Immediate high-and-tight tourniquet if unsure of injury level. Otherwise 2-3 inches proximal. Direct pressure while preparing tourniquet. Elevate above heart. Do not waste time searching for specific pressure points. Speed is critical.

Act on spurting bleed:
1. Apply high-and-tight tourniquet immediately
2. Maintain direct pressure while preparing device
3. Elevate above heart without searching pressure points
CPU: 70%, RAM: 59%, Temp: 70°C, Battery: 13%, LoRa RSSI: -119dBm, SNR: -8dB
CPU 70°C, RAM 59%, Bat 13%, RSSI -119dBm bad.
New nodes join by matching existing network parameters: Region EU_868, Channel settings including frequency slot 1, PSK encryption key, and Long_Slow modem preset. Reboot required.

Sync network parameters:
1. Set Region EU_868
2. Match channel frequency slot
3. Enter identical PSK key
4. Set Long_Slow preset
5. Reboot to join
Wring out moss or grass. Place near body heat. Use inside of bark. Cedar or juniper bark resists water. Feather sticks from dry interior. Keep tinder in waterproof bag.

Use inner bark:
1. Wring dry moss
2. Body heat warm
3. Cedar/juniper bark
4. Keep in dry bag
Scrape magnesium shavings into pile. Strike ferro rod into shavings. Magnesium burns hot 5000F. Ignites wet tinder. Use knife back to scrape. Shavings ignite easily.

Shave then strike:
1. Scrape magnesium pile
2. Spark into shavings
3. Burns 5000F
4. Ignites wet tinder
Codes: SOS=distress, X=medical, V=need help, Y=yes, N=no, arrow=proceeding direction. 3m size.

Emergency codes:
1. SOS: grave danger
2. X: medical needed
3. V: assistance required
4. Arrow: direction traveling
5. Y/N: yes/no answers
Child over 1 year: Give 5 back blows between shoulder blades with heel of hand. Then 5 abdominal thrusts. Alternate until breathing resumes or help arrives. Call emergency if continues.

For child:
1. 5 back blows
2. 5 belly thrusts
3. Alternate until clear
4. Call emergency if persists
Boiling is more reliable against all pathogens including Cryptosporidium cysts. Bleach is less effective against some protozoa. Boil for 1 minute minimum; bleach requires 30 min contact time.

Boiling wins:
1. Kills everything including cysts
2. Bleach misses some protozoa
3. Boil: immediate use
4. Bleach: 30 min wait
Boil when possible
Obese victim: Perform chest thrusts rather than abdominal. Place hands on lower sternum. Push backward. Alternate with back blows until airway clears.

Large victim:
1. Chest thrusts only
2. Hands on sternum
3. Push backward
4. Alternate with back slaps
Solar signal device: Aim flash using hand or hole. Range 10km+. 3 quick flashes indicate distress.
Reflect sun for rescue: 3 flashes at cockpit. Range 10-20km.
Pilot protocol: SOS ground symbol 3m+. Mirror flashes. Orange smoke. Wave arms overhead in semaphore.

Helicopter SOS:
1. Large SOS on ground
2. Mirror aimed at pilot
3. Three orange smoke puffs
4. Arms raised overhead
5. Stay in open
Transmitted 28 bytes. RSSI: -82 dBm. 2 hops to Node B.
Message relayed to Node B. Signal strong.
SOS continuous beacon. Source bearing 240 deg.
SOS beacon 240 deg. Continuous.
Lethal dose 4000 mSv acute. Evacuate at 1000 µSv/h sustained. Early symptoms at 1000 mSv. Acute radiation syndrome threshold 2000 mSv whole body.

Lethal levels:
1. 4000 mSv = LD50/60
2. Evacuate at 1000 µSv/h
3. ARS begins at 2000 mSv
4. Early nausea at 1000 mSv
Scrape magnesium or ferro shavings directly into tinder bundle. Use knife spine to scrape wood curls. Use inner bark of cedar or birch. Char cloth substitute: punk wood.

Make instant tinder:
1. Scrape rod into curls
2. Wood shavings with spine
3. Cedar inner bark
4. Punk wood char
Relayed to Alpha. Direct link, strong signal.
Relayed to Alpha. Direct link established.
Common Mallow (Malva neglecta). Leaves, seeds, flowers edible. Mucilaginous like okra. Cheese-shaped fruit segments.
Mallow. Leaves, seeds edible. Mucilaginous texture.

Assisting friend with forearm cut: Have them lie down if possible. You apply pressure with gloved hands or dressing. Elevate their arm above their heart. If blood soaks through, add layers. Apply tourniquet to their upper arm 5 cm above wound if bleeding continues. Reassure patient.

Help friend with forearm cut:
1. Apply pressure with gloved hands while they recline
2. Elevate their arm above heart level
3. Tourniquet upper arm 5 cm above wound if soaking through
Sent. Node B acknowledged. Safe journey.
Sent to Node B. Acknowledged.
Shock position: Supine with legs elevated 30-45 degrees or Trendelenburg. Improves cerebral perfusion. Keep warm, monitor vital signs.

Position:
1. Supine flat
2. Legs 45deg up
3. Warm cover
4. Monitor signs
CPU: 34%, RAM: 42%, Temp: 62°C, Battery: 82%, LoRa RSSI: -84dBm, SNR: 5.5dB
CPU 62°C, RAM 42%, Bat 82%, RSSI -84dBm.
SOS repeater detected. Signal hopping.
SOS repeater hopping. Signal unstable.
No SOS pattern detected. Channel noise floor -110dBm.
Clear. No SOS in 5s window.
Boiling is fastest reliable: 1-3 min. UV pens 90 seconds but need batteries. Chemicals take 30 min to 4 hours contact time.
Boiling: 1-3 min. UV pen 90 sec if charged. Chemicals: 30 min wait.
Reflector wall: Stack rocks 3ft high in crescent shape behind fire opening toward shelter. Dry rocks only. Wall reflects heat 10ft. Lean-to behind wall gets maximum benefit.

Stack rock wall:
1. Crescent 3ft high
2. Dry rocks only
3. Open to shelter
4. Fire 6ft from wall
Enable pairing on both: Triple-click user button. Ensure 868.4 MHz frequency and SHORT_SLOW preset align.
Triple-click to pair. Use 868.4 MHz and SHORT_SLOW preset. Align channel settings exactly.
Shock indicators: Pale clammy skin, rapid weak pulse, cold extremities. Treatment: Lay casualty flat, elevate legs 6-12 inches, maintain body heat, monitor breathing. Do not give fluids.

Shock protocol:
1. Lay flat
2. Raise legs 30cm
3. Keep warm
4. Monitor pulse
Solomon's Seal (Polygonatum biflorum). Root edible cooked. Berries toxic. Arching stem distinctive.
Solomon seal. Root edible cooked. Berries toxic.
Minimize breaks. Switch compressors every 2 minutes if available. Push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. 30:2 ratio. No rest otherwise.

Minimize rest:
1. Switch q 2 min
2. Push 5-6cm
3. 100-120 bpm
4. 30:2 ratio
5. No pauses
Clear water-filled plastic bag shaped as lens. Eyeglasses convex lens. Ice lens carved from clear ice. Camera lens or binoculars.
Focus sunlight. 1. Water bag: shape as convex lens. 2. Eyeglasses: use convex side. 3. Ice: carve clear block into lens.
Wind: Keep ridgeline low 2-3ft to reduce profile. Rain: Higher 4ft for headroom. Storm: Very low 18 inches with tarp staked directly to ground on windward side.

Keep low in wind:
1. Ridgeline 2-3ft high
2. Stake windward edge
3. Sleep on lee side
4. Guy out all corners

Direct strike hits person. Side flash jumps from struck object (tree) to person nearby. Both lethal. Side flash radius 15-20 meters. Ground current spreads from strike point. All require immediate shelter protocol.

Both lethal:
1. Direct hits person
2. Side flash jumps 15-20m
3. Ground current spreads
4. Stay 20m from objects
Northern Dewberry (Rubus flagellaris). Trailing vine, black berries. Edible. Earlier than blackberries. Prickly stems.
Dewberry. Trailing vine. Black edible fruit. Prickly.
Field CPR: 30:2 ratio, 5-6cm depth, 100-120 bpm. Continue until exhaustion. No AED may be available for extended periods in remote areas.

Field protocol:
1. 30 compressions hard
2. 2 rescue breaths
3. 5-6cm depth
4. 100-120 rate
5. Continue 30+ min if needed
No. Coffee filters remove debris and some parasites (Giardia cysts) but miss bacteria and viruses. Must follow with boiling or chemical treatment. Good for pre-filtering only.

Coffee filter insufficient:
1. Removes debris only
2. Catches some cysts
3. Bacteria pass through
4. Viruses pass through
Always disinfect after filtering
CPU: 24%, RAM: 33%, Temp: 44°C, Battery: 86%, LoRa RSSI: -74dBm, SNR: 8dB
Temp 44°C, Battery 86%, RSSI -74dBm.

Results: Not safe. Warning means voltage dropped below 4.65V. Pi is throttling (600MHz). SD card writes may corrupt. USB devices reset. Continuing risks filesystem damage and data loss. Fix immediately.

Not safe despite apparent stability:
1. CPU throttled to 600MHz (hidden performance loss)
2. SD card corruption imminent under writes
3. Fix now: replace PSU and cable before data loss
Unstable 5V rail from PSU sag or cable inductance. Fix: 5.1V fixed voltage supply, short thick cable (20AWG), add 1000uF capacitor across 5V/GPIO if marginal.
Unstable 5V rail. Fix: 1. 5.1V fixed supply 2. Short 20AWG cable 3. Add 1000uF cap if marginal
Management: Control bleeding first if present, then flat position, leg elevation, warmth, oxygen if available, rapid evacuation.

Trauma shock:
1. Stop bleeding
2. Flat position
3. Legs up
4. Evacuate
Emergency sent. Base responding with ETA 45 min.
Emergency sent. Base responding, ETA 45 min.
Emergency TX sent
SOS transmitted with position.
10W insufficient for 24/7 operation. Minimum 15W required even with aggressive power management. Risk of deep discharge in cloudy weather.

10 W insufficient:
1. Minimum 15 W required
2. Even with power saving
3. Risk deep discharge
4. Use 20 W for safety
Back blows technique: Position victim leaning forward. Strike firmly between shoulder blades with heel of hand 5 times. Check if object dislodged after each set.

Back blows:
1. Lean them forward
2. Heel of hand
3. Strike between shoulders
4. Repeat 5 times

Deep dry caves are safe. Avoid cave entrances and wet walls. Stay 20m from entrance walls. Drip lines conduct. Shallow overhangs unsafe. If cave is wet or small, descend instead. 30/30 rule still applies after exit.

Cave rules:
1. Deep and dry only
2. Stay 20m from entrance
3. Avoid wet walls
4. Shallow overhangs unsafe
CPU: 56%, RAM: 42%, Temp: 56°C, Battery: 73%, LoRa RSSI: -80dBm, SNR: 5dB
Temp 56°C, Battery 73%, RSSI -80dBm.
Enable pairing: triple-press user button. Match modem preset (LONG_FAST) and frequency (868.0 MHz). Channel key must match exactly.
Triple-press user button. Match frequency 868 MHz and modem preset exactly. Wait 30s.
Conscious choking: If partial obstruction encourage coughing. For complete blockage administer 5 back blows between shoulders then 5 abdominal thrusts. Repeat sequence until clear.

Complete blockage:
1. 5 back slaps
2. 5 abdominal thrusts
3. Repeat sequence
4. Call 911 if needed
CPU: 55%, RAM: 40%, Temp: 55°C, Battery: 93%, LoRa RSSI: -71dBm, SNR: 11.2dB
CPU 55°C, Bat 93%, RSSI -71dBm SNR 11dB strong.
Coltsfoot (Tussilago farfara). Flowers edible. Leaves edible cooked. Pyrrolizidine alkaloids - limit use.
Coltsfoot. Flowers edible. Limit due alkaloids.
HQ informed.
Warning at <4.63V. Common: 2m 26AWG cable drops 0.5V. Fix: Use 5.1V supply, 18AWG 0.8m max, avoid USB extensions, use powered hub for peripherals.
Warning at <4.63V. Fix: 1. 5.1V supply 2. 18AWG max 0.8m 3. No extensions
Alone: 30 compressions (5-6cm, 100-120 bpm) then 2 breaths. Call for help first if possible. Continue until exhausted or help arrives.

Solo method:
1. Call help first
2. 30 compressions
3. 2 breaths
4. 5-6cm deep
5. 100-120 bpm
ALERT: Flood warning from node 5.
Alert: Flood warning node 5.
A-frame: Ridgeline between two trees at 4ft. Third tree supports front pole creating porch roof. Stake sides low. Dig trench uphill for runoff. Sheds heavy rain.

Set A-frame:
1. Ridgeline between two trees
2. Front pole to third tree
3. Stake sides low
4. Dig trench uphill
Binding procedure: Set identical region EU_868. Configure matching channel slot and PSK. Use same modem preset (Long_Fast). Save settings. Reboot. Node appears in list within 60 seconds.

Configure then reboot:
1. Set Region EU_868
2. Match channel slot and PSK
3. Use Long_Fast preset
4. Save configuration
5. Reboot and verify
USB-C resistance or PD negotiation failure. Use 5.1V fixed supply (not PD), 20AWG cable, ensure CC pins connected. Avoid e-marked cables with Pi5.
USB-C resistance high. Fix: 1. 5.1V fixed supply 2. 20AWG cable 3. Avoid e-marked cables
Quick aid: Flat position, legs up 12 inches, warmth, reassurance. Check pulse. Do not move unnecessarily.

Quick aid:
1. Flat down
2. Legs up
3. Warmth
4. Reassure
Switch every 2 minutes or 5 cycles. Minimize pause during switch <5 seconds. Push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. 30:2 ratio.

Rotate q 2 min:
1. Switch 5 cycles
2. <5 sec pause
3. Push 5-6cm
4. 100-120 bpm
5. 30:2 ratio

Surface temperature above 45°C indicates high internal resistance or short circuit. Safe operating skin temp: <45°C. Internal temp likely 50-60°C. Stop discharge immediately. Allow cooling before handling.
Hot surface >45°C unsafe. Stop use. Cool before handling. Check for shorts.
Symptoms: Pale cold skin, clammy texture, rapid shallow breathing, weak pulse. Aid: Horizontal position, leg elevation, warmth, no oral intake.

Signs confirmed:
1. Flat on back
2. Raise legs 12in
3. Thermal blanket
4. Watch breathing
Summit report sent.
Detection: Verify HAT seated properly. Check SPI enabled. Verify /dev/spidev0.0 present. Check power supply adequate. Ensure HAT compatible with Pi 5 voltage levels.

Fix detection:
1. Reseat HAT
2. Enable SPI
3. Check spidev0.0
4. Verify power
5. Check voltage
Emergency compressions: 5-6cm depth, 100-120 bpm, 30:2 ratio. Continue until help arrives. Only intervention for cardiac arrest.

Last resort:
1. Push 5-6cm
2. 100-120 bpm
3. 30:2 cycles
4. Only chance
5. Don't stop
Solid warning = severe undervoltage <4.5V. Risk of SD corruption. Immediate: use 5.1V/5A PSU, 0.5m 18AWG cable, check for loose connections. Avoid GPIO power if possible.
Severe <4.5V risk corruption. Fix: 1. 5.1V/5A PSU 2. 0.5m 18AWG 3. Check connections
Rocky ground conducts current through cracks. Wet rock increases risk. Avoid cave entrances and overhangs. Current arcs across wet surfaces. Find lower ground.
Wet rock conducts current. Avoid cave mouths. Current arcs across wet surfaces. Descend now.
High risk of parasites and algae. Pre-filter essential through cloth. Boil vigorously 5 minutes minimum. Avoid drinking if possible.
High parasite risk. Avoid if possible. If forced: filter, boil hard 5 min.
On cloudy days, extend exposure to 2 consecutive days or 6 hours of cumulative sunlight. If rain interferes, restart timing. Water must be clear for UV penetration.

Cloudy weather protocol:
1. Expose 2 full days
2. Or 6 hrs cumulative sun
3. Water must be clear
4. Rain restarts timer
Manual: Lock elbows, heel of hand on sternum, push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. 30:2 ratio. Body weight over hands.

Manual method:
1. Lock elbows
2. Heel on sternum
3. Push 5-6cm
4. 100-120 bpm
5. Body weight down
Emergency broadcast. 7 nodes in mesh acknowledged.
Flood warning broadcast. 7 nodes acknowledged.
With blade: Carve feather sticks from dead standing wood for tinder. Shape spindle and hearth board for bow drill. Scrape magnesium or use knife spine to strike flint against char cloth.
Carve components first. 1. Feather sticks: shave curls from dry branch. 2. Bow drill: carve spindle and hearth, use bootlace. 3. Flint strike: hit rock with knife spine onto tinder.
Lethal 4 Sv (4000 mSv) whole body. RDD unlikely to achieve lethal rates except at ground zero. Evacuate >1 mSv/h. Contamination primary hazard, not acute dose.

Lethal parameters:
1. 4 Sv whole body lethal
2. RDD rarely lethal acutely
3. >1 mSv/h evacuate zone
4. Contamination > radiation burns
Steps: 1) Lay flat 2) Raise legs 12 inches 3) Loosen clothing 4) Keep warm 5) Do not give fluids 6) Monitor vital signs.

Treatment:
1. Lay flat
2. Raise legs
3. Loosen clothes
4. Keep warm
CPU: 31%, RAM: 52%, Temp: 60°C, Battery: 31%, LoRa RSSI: -106dBm, SNR: -5dB
Temp 60°C, Battery 31%, RSSI -106dBm weak.
Unconscious: Lower to ground. Call emergency. Begin CPR. 30 chest compressions. Open airway. Look for object. Remove if visible. 2 rescue breaths. Repeat.

If unconscious:
1. Start CPR
2. Check mouth for object
3. Remove if seen
4. Continue cycles
Raised platform: Four Y-sticks in ground two poles across as frame sticks laid perpendicular to create bed. Height 1ft above water. Roof tarp overhead.

Raise platform:
1. Four Y-sticks
2. Cross poles on forks
3. Lay bed sticks
4. Height 1ft above wet
TX confirmed
Vehicle found reported.
Never lie flat. Ground current spreads horizontally and can kill. Instead, squat with feet together, heels touching. Minimizes ground contact. Ball of feet only. Cover ears from thunder shock wave.

Never lie flat:
1. Ground current spreads
2. Squat with feet together
3. Minimize ground contact
4. Cover ears
Management: 1) Lay flat 2) Raise legs 30cm 3) Keep warm with blanket 4) Loosen tight clothing 5) Monitor pulse and breathing.

Immediate action:
1. Flat on ground
2. Raise legs
3. Keep warm
4. Check pulse
Use knife spine to strike flint or quartz rocks creating sparks. Carve feather sticks for tinder. If battery present, use blade to hold steel wool against terminals.
Use spine and tinder. 1. Flint strike: scrape quartz with knife spine. 2. Feather sticks: curl dry wood shavings. 3. Battery assist: bridge 9V with blade-held wool.
Seek dense building: concrete or brick. Avoid top floors. Seal all openings. Stay 12-24 hours minimum for short-lived isotopes to decay. Limit time outside.

Seek dense structure:
1. Concrete/brick building preferred
2. Seal cracks with wet cloth
3. Avoid top floors
4. Wait 24h before brief outside exposure
Yes, hand drill method uses spindle and hearth board only. No cord needed. Requires dry softwood hearth and harder spindle. Technique is physically demanding.
Yes, hand drill only. 1. Spindle: hard stick. 2. Hearth: soft wood board. 3. Spin with palms until ember forms in notch.

VTC6: 300 cycles at 15A. 3120mAh. Check voltage sag.
Iodine tablets 30 min. Chlorine dioxide 4 hours. UV pen 90 sec. Solar disinfection 6 hours clear bottle. Cloth alone insufficient.

Results: Target <0.2V drop at 2.5A for safety margin. At 3A with 0.2Ω cable, drop is 0.6V (4.4V at Pi). Use Ohm's Law: R = V/I. For 0.2V drop at 3A, R <0.067Ω. 24AWG wire is 0.084Ω/m, so max 0.8m cable length.

Max 0.2V drop acceptable:
1. Calculate: Drop = Current × Resistance
2. Use 20AWG or thicker for runs >0.5m
3. Measure drop: V_at_Pi = V_supply - (I × R_cable)

Offline fix: Check 868.1 MHz match. Hold button 7s to reset pairing. Use LONG_SLOW preset.
Check 868.1 MHz match. Hold 7s to reset. Use LONG_SLOW preset for link.
SOS transmitted. Medical team notified via Node C.
SOS sent. Medical team notified via Node C.
SOS broadcast. 3 nodes acknowledged. Relay active.
SOS broadcast active. 3 nodes acknowledged.
CPU: 47%, RAM: 52%, Temp: 47°C, Battery: 24%, LoRa RSSI: -100dBm, SNR: -2dB
CPU 47°C, Bat 24%, RX -100dBm SNR -2dB weak.
Adding Mobile-Unit: Set region EU_868. Configure channel to match mesh with correct PSK. Use Long_Fast modem preset. Save. Reboot. Unit joins and appears in list.

Add Mobile-Unit:
1. Set EU_868
2. Match channel
3. Enter PSK
4. Use Long_Fast
5. Reboot

Cell phones do not attract lightning. Metal in phone is too small. However, holding phone to ear increases head exposure if struck. Put phone away. Focus on descent and shelter. Bluetooth is irrelevant.

Phone not attractor:
1. Metal too small
2. But put it away
3. Head exposure risk
4. Focus on descent
Lithium batteries can short against steel wool but are volatile and dangerous. Safer to use 9V alkaline. Phone batteries may overheat or rupture when shorted.
Risky with LiPo. 1. Use 9V alkaline instead. 2. If forced, briefly touch wool to wires. 3. Have water ready, risk of explosion.
Count seconds between flash and thunder. Divide by 3 for km. If result is ≤10 km (30 seconds), shelter immediately. If ≤5 seconds (1.6 km), extreme danger. Strikes can occur 10+ km from storm edge.

Count method:
1. Flash-to-boom ÷ 3 = km
2. ≤30s means ≤10km
3. ≤5s is extreme danger
4. Shelter at 30s threshold
CPU: 19%, RAM: 29%, Temp: 49°C, Battery: 15%, LoRa RSSI: -88dBm, SNR: 4dB
Temp 49°C, Battery 15%, Signal -88dBm.
Configuration: Mount module on Pi. Enable SPI. Install Meshtastic. Set region EU_868. Define channel name and PSK. Verify radio initialization successful.

Configure radio:
1. Mount module
2. Enable SPI
3. Install Meshtastic
4. Set EU_868
5. Verify init
Standard strength 130 mg potassium iodide. Take upon sheltering if radioiodine suspected. Single tablet per day. Crush and mix with liquid if swallowing difficult. Store dry.

Tablet specs:
1. Standard strength 130 mg
2. One tablet per 24h
3. Crushable if needed
4. Take upon sheltering
CPU: 68%, RAM: 65%, Temp: 68°C, Battery: 33%, LoRa RSSI: -97dBm, SNR: 1dB
CPU 68°C warm, RAM 65%, Bat 33%, RSSI -97dBm.
Pale cold victim: Shock indicators present. Position flat, elevate lower limbs, prevent further heat loss, monitor vital signs.

Pale and cold:
1. Flat position
2. Lower limbs up
3. Prevent cold
4. Monitor vitals
Check pulse every 2 minutes. Minimize interruption <10 seconds. Push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. 30:2 ratio. Continue if no pulse.

Pulse checks:
1. Every 2 minutes
2. <10 seconds
3. Push 5-6cm
4. 100-120 bpm
5. Continue if absent
Dense structure shielding. Close fireplace dampers. Seal gaps with tape. Duration 48h typical. Centrally located room on middle floor preferred over basement if wet.

Bare minimum shelter:
1. Dense structure middle floor
2. Close fireplace dampers
3. Tape all gaps/seams
4. Wait 48h for decay
Open terrain: crouch on insulating material. Keep feet together to minimize ground current. Remove metal objects. Do not lie flat. Wait for storm passage.
Crouch on pack/insulation. Feet together. Drop metal. Do not lie flat. Wait.
Descend immediately when 30-second flash-to-bang observed. Do not delay for photos or gear. Strikes frequent on high points. Speed over comfort.
Descend immediately at 30s flash-to-bang. No delays. Strikes frequent on high points. Speed first.
Depth: 5-6cm (2 inches). Speed: 100-120 compressions per minute. Allow full recoil. 30 compressions to 2 breaths ratio.

Hard and fast:
1. 5-6cm depth
2. 100-120 bpm
3. Full recoil
4. 30:2 ratio
5. Continuous

Pack temperature limits dictated by hottest cell. Ambient 45°C with charging can push internal cells to 60°C+. Derate charging current above 35°C ambient. Stop discharge if pack exceeds 50°C surface temp.
Pack <50°C surface. Derate charge >35°C ambient. Stop at 60°C internal.
URGENT flag detected from node 12. Message follows.
Urgent traffic from node 12. Standing by.
Visual distress: SOS requires 3m symbols, high contrast to terrain. Place in open area. Effective range 3-8km depending on aircraft altitude.
Visible SOS: 3m minimum, high contrast, open ground. Aircraft see 4-8km.
Wild Strawberry (Fragaria vesca). White flowers, trifoliate leaves. Fruit sweet, edible. Yellow seeds.
Wild Strawberry. Edible. White flowers, yellow seeds.
Safe distance where dose <100 µSv/h. Time outdoors limited to hours not days. Distance squared law applies. Shielding less effective than distance for gamma.

Safe parameters:
1. <100 µSv/h considered safe
2. Double distance = quarter dose
3. Gamma requires mass shielding
4. Minimize time in zone
Windpipe blockage: Immediate back blows. Then abdominal thrusts. Alternate. Do not attempt blind finger sweeps in mouth. Call 911 if persists.

Windpipe blocked:
1. Back blows x5
2. Thrusts x5
3. No finger sweeps
4. Call 911
Check sent. HQ replied: Signal 5 by 5.
Radio check sent. HQ reports signal 5 by 5.
Geometry: Straight lines, sharp angles. Minimum 3m segments. Contrast with background. No curves.

Signal geometry:
1. Straight lines only
2. Sharp 90° angles
3. 3 meter segments minimum
4. No curves or circles
5. Clear block letters
Partial obstruction: Encourage coughing. Do NOT interfere if breathing adequate. Monitor closely. If worsens to complete blockage begin back blows and thrusts immediately.

Partial blockage:
1. Encourage coughing
2. Do not interfere
3. Monitor closely
4. Act if worsens
Hard-top vehicles provide Faraday cage protection. Do not touch metal frame. Avoid convertibles. Current flows around exterior. Stay inside until storm passes.
Hard-top cars safe. Do not touch metal. Current flows around exterior. Stay inside. Avoid convertibles.
Re-pair both devices: Triple-press button. Check 868.7 MHz, LONG_FAST preset. Maximum range 8km line-of-sight.
Triple-press to re-pair. Check 868.7 MHz and LONG_FAST. Stay within 8km range.
Hand drill uses a straight spindle rotated rapidly between palms against a hearth board. Requires softwood hearth and harder spindle. Downward pressure and speed create hot ember in notch.
Rotate stick by hand. 1. Cut notch in soft hearth board. 2. Place spindle tip in notch, spin with palms. 3. Blow hot ember onto tinder bundle.
Heat stones in fire until very hot (not river stones which may explode). Drop into water container. Bring to boil. Requires many stones and heat-resistant vessel. Alternative when no pot over fire.

Hot stone method:
1. Heat stones in fire (not wet ones)
2. Drop into water vessel
3. Repeat until boiling
4. Requires heat-proof container
Primitive but effective
Cardiac massage: Push 5-6cm deep at 100-120 bpm on lower sternum. 30:2 ratio. Heel of hand with locked elbows.

Massage heart:
1. Lower sternum
2. Push 5-6cm
3. 100-120 bpm
4. 30:2 ratio
5. Locked elbows
Eastern White Pine (Pinus strobus). Needles vitamin C rich. Safe tea. Avoid yew (toxic lookalike).
White Pine. Needles for vitamin C tea. Avoid yew.
6 hours of strong sun provides cumulative UV-A dose of ~550 Wh/m² needed to inactivate bacteria and viruses. Less time may leave viable pathogens. Cloudy weather requires 2 days equivalent.

6 hour minimum:
1. 550 Wh/m² UV-A dose needed
2. Kills bacteria/viruses
3. Less time leaves risks
4. Cloudy = 2 days
Cumulative exposure matters
2 mSv/h requires immediate evacuation. At this rate, reach 50 mSv emergency limit in 25 hours. Move crosswind to increase distance. Seek dense building if cannot evacuate.

Evacuate immediately:
1. 2 mSv/h = 50 mSv in 25h
2. Move crosswind immediately
3. Seek dense structure if trapped
4. Cover nose/mouth with cloth
Slow pull removes material. Fast pull creates heat and sparks. Speed matters more than pressure. 45 degree angle optimal. Push through the stroke. Full length of rod.

Fast for heat:
1. Quick pull
2. 45 degrees
3. Full stroke
4. Speed over pressure
Boiling sterilizes. Freezing keeps it sterile indefinitely. Store in clean container. Thaw completely before drinking. Good for stockpiling.
Boil then freeze keeps sterile. Use clean vessel. Thaw fully before consuming.

Emergency arterial arm wound care: Recognize spurting blood. Immediate tourniquet application is often life-saving. Place high on upper arm (as proximal as possible) or 2-3 inches above wound. Tighten until spurting stops. Mark time. Cover with dressing after bleeding controlled.

Treat arterial arm wound:
1. Apply tourniquet high on upper arm immediately
2. Tighten until spurting blood stops completely
3. Mark time and cover with dressing after control
30/30 rule: seek shelter if flash-to-bang is 30 seconds or less. Stay sheltered 30 minutes after final thunder. Each 3 seconds equals roughly 1 kilometer.
30/30 rule: shelter if <=30s flash-to-bang. Stay 30min after last thunder. 3s = 1km.
Emergency sent. Fire team dispatched to your grid.
Fire alert sent. Fire team dispatched.
Measured 4.5V at Pi with 5V at PSU. Cable loss 0.5V. Fix: Shorter cable (<0.5m), thicker gauge (18AWG), 5.1V PSU setting, reduce load by moving SSD to powered hub.
Cable loss 0.5V. Fix: 1. <0.5m cable 2. 18AWG 3. 5.1V setting 4. Powered hub
2 breaths after 30 compressions. Each breath 1 second. Compress 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm. 30:2 ratio standard.

2 breaths:
1. After 30 pumps
2. 1 second each
3. Chest rise
4. 5-6cm depth
5. 100-120 bpm
Emergency TX
Water SOS sent.
Broadcast on mesh. 5 nodes within range acknowledged.
Shelter location broadcast. 5 nodes acknowledged.
Use jumper cables or wire. Connect to terminals and touch ends to steel wool. Sparks will fly. Ensure good contact. Risk of shock. 12V can ignite fine steel wool.

Wire to terminals:
1. Jumper cable clips
2. Touch to steel wool
3. Sparks ignite
4. Watch for shock
Mirror rescue: Direct sun reflection at aircraft. Sighting hole assists aim. Visibility 12km. 3 flashes = help.
Mirror: Aim at pilot using reflection hole. 3 flashes. Max 16km.
Contains oil that burns wet. Peel from tree or ground. Shred into strips. Ignite with spark or flame. Burns black smoke. Excellent tinder even when damp.

Oil burns wet:
1. Peel white bark
2. Shred to strips
3. Takes spark easy
4. Burns when damp
CPU: 66%, RAM: 81%, Temp: 66°C, Battery: 11%, LoRa RSSI: -115dBm, SNR: -7dB
CPU 66°C, RAM 81%, Bat 11% low, RSSI -115dBm.
Evacuate if >1 mSv/h. Limit exposure to 10 mSv for life-saving, 50 mSv for emergency operations. Time outdoors inversely proportional to dose rate.

Flee indicators:
1. >1 mSv/h mandatory evacuation
2. 10 mSv max for rescue ops
3. 50 mSv absolute emergency limit
4. Increase distance rapidly
Emergency: Lay flat immediately, elevate legs 30 degrees, conserve body heat, monitor for deteriorating consciousness, prepare evacuation.

Emergency:
1. Flat now
2. Legs up 30deg
3. Warm cover
4. Monitor mind
Brown seed head fluffy. Highly flammable. Explosive flash. Collect in dry weather. Holds spark well. Mix with grass for longer burn. Seasonal availability.

Brown heads fluffy:
1. Highly flammable
2. Explosive flash
3. Mix with grass
4. Seasonal find

Likely self-heating from the voltage regulator on the breakout board or proximity to the Pi 5 CPU. Also check if running in continuous mode. Remedy: use forced mode (single shot), increase distance from board, ensure 3.3V operation.

Self-heating from regulator or CPU.
1. Enable forced mode in driver
2. Move sensor >5cm from Pi board
3. Confirm 3.3V supply voltage
4. Add airflow gap
CPU: 41%, RAM: 35%, Temp: 41°C, Battery: 100%, LoRa RSSI: -64dBm, SNR: 12dB
Temp 41°C, Battery 100%, RSSI -64dBm excellent.
Connect to car battery. Touch ends to steel wool. Heavy spark risk. Wear gloves. 12V high current. Instant ignition. Keep cables apart to avoid short.

Heavy spark risk:
1. Connect to battery
2. Touch to wool
3. Wear gloves
4. Avoid short circuit
15W panel absolute minimum, 25W practical. Use 35Wh 3S 18650 with protection circuit. PWM works but MPPT recommended for marginal conditions.

Minimum 25 W practical:
1. 15 W absolute min
2. 25 W recommended
3. 35 Wh 3S pack
4. MPPT preferred
Bracken Fern (Pteridium aquilinum). Carcinogenic compound ptaquiloside. Must boil 15 min change water. Limit intake.
Bracken. Toxic raw. Boil 15 min. Eat sparingly.
Crossing confirmed.
End-of-life typically defined as 70-80% of nominal capacity. Consumer grade: 300 cycles. Industrial grade: 1000+ cycles. Calendar aging reduces capacity 2-3% per year regardless of use.
Consumer: 300 cycles. Industrial: 1000+. Replace at 70% capacity.
Cut foil or gum wrapper into thin hourglass shape. Touch to battery ends. Acts as resistor heating up. Ignites quickly. Have tinder ready. AA or 9V works.

Foil hourglass:
1. Thin middle section
2. Touch battery ends
3. Heats to ignite
4. Ready tinder
Loading dose 130 mg potassium iodide. Saturates thyroid within 1 hour. Protection lasts 24 hours. Re-dose every 24 hours if exposure continues. Take with food to reduce stomach upset.

Loading protocol:
1. 130 mg saturates thyroid
2. Protection active 1h post
3. Lasts 24 hours
4. Re-dose daily if exposure continues
Store Li-ion at 15°C-25°C for longevity. Short term: -20°C to 45°C acceptable. Never store above 60°C or below -40°C. Store at 40-50% charge state for extended periods.
Store 15-25°C at 40-50% charge. Never exceed 60°C.
Small object: Treat same as any choking. 5 back blows. 5 thrusts. Object may fly out on first blow. Check breathing after each attempt.

Small object:
1. 5 back blows
2. 5 thrusts
3. Check breathing
4. Repeat if needed
Sent 38 bytes. ACK received from Base.
Coordinates transmitted to Base. Acknowledged.
Warning broadcast.
Put both in pairing mode. Verify 868 MHz, modem preset MEDIUM_SLOW, and encryption key 0x01. Reboot after config.
Enable pairing mode. Verify 868.0 MHz and MEDIUM_SLOW preset match. Reboot both units.
Hen of the Woods (Grifola frondosa). Clustered fronds at tree base. Edible. Polypore. No gills. Pores underside.
Hen of Woods. Cluster at base. Frond-like. Polypore.
CPU: 28%, RAM: 51%, Temp: 48°C, Battery: 23%, LoRa RSSI: -91dBm, SNR: 2.1dB
Temp 48°C, Battery 23%, RSSI -91dBm SNR 2dB.
Relayed via Node D. Command acknowledged.
Protocol: Lay casualty supine, elevate legs 30-45cm using stable support, cover with blanket, ensure clear airway, monitor continuously.

Position protocol:
1. Supine flat
2. Legs 45cm up
3. Blanket cover
4. Airway clear
First steps: Mount HAT on Pi. Enable SPI. Install Meshtastic software. Set region EU_868. Configure primary channel with PSK. Check device recognizes radio.

Initial setup:
1. Mount HAT on Pi
2. Enable SPI
3. Install Meshtastic
4. Set EU_868
5. Configure channel
Undervoltage detected at 4.65V. Causes: cable resistance >0.3ohm, PSU sag under load. Solutions: 5.1V PSU, <1m 20AWG cable, powered USB hub for peripherals.
Voltage drop in cable. Fix: 1. Use <1m 20AWG USB-C 2. 5.1V/5A PSU 3. Powered hub for SSD
Clear channel. No emergency beacons.
Channel clear. No beacons.
Snow harbors bacteria from animal contact. Must be boiled 3-5 minutes at altitude. Do not eat snow directly as it lowers core temperature.
Boil snowmelt 3-5 min. Never eat snow direct. Kills bacteria from animal contact.
Alum causes sediment to flocculate and settle rapidly. Clarifies but does not sterilize. Decant clear water then boil before consumption.
Alum clears sediment by flocculation. Not sterilization. Decant then boil water.
Can be shaped into hook or used as striker. Not effective alone. Better used as bearing block for bow drill or to short battery if conductive.
Limited use. 1. Use as socket in bow drill. 2. Bend to hold wool for battery. 3. Not effective as standalone sparker.
Common Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale). Root, leaf, flower edible. Bitter. High minerals.
Dandelion. Root, leaf, flower edible. Boil roots.
Pasteurization occurs at 70°C for 1 minute (WAPI indicator). Boiling (100°C) is standard. Some viruses die at 60°C, but cysts require 70°C+ for several minutes. Bring to rolling boil to be safe.

Heat requirements:
1. 70°C for 1 min pasteurizes
2. 100°C boil is standard
3. Cysts need higher heat longer
4. Rolling boil ensures safety
Outpost-3 missing: Verify EU_868 region set. Check channel PSK matches mesh. Confirm Long_Fast preset used. Check power and antenna. May be out of range or in sleep mode.

Debug Outpost-3:
1. Check EU_868
2. Verify PSK
3. Confirm preset
4. Check power/antenna
5. Check sleep mode
Cloudberry (Rubus chamaemorus). Amber berries when ripe. Cloud-like leaves. Rich flavor. Safe. Bog plant.
Cloudberry. Amber when ripe. Cloud leaves. Bog plant.

Discard when capacity falls below 70% of rated capacity or internal resistance doubles from new. Typical lifespan 3-5 years or 500 cycles. High drain applications retire at 300 cycles due to voltage sag.
Trash at 70% capacity or 2x resistance. Typical: 500 cycles.
Setup: Set Relay-Node to EU_868. Match channel configuration including PSK. Use Long_Fast modem preset. Enable router role if desired. Save and reboot.

Setup Relay-Node:
1. Set EU_868
2. Match channel config
3. Input PSK
4. Use Long_Fast preset
5. Reboot
Debug steps: Verify Node Alpha uses EU_868 region. Check channel PSK identical to mesh. Confirm modem preset matches. Check antenna and power. Ensure not in deep sleep or offline mode.

Debug Node Alpha:
1. Check EU_868 region
2. Verify PSK matches
3. Confirm modem preset
4. Check antenna/power
5. Exit offline mode
Layer gravel, sand, charcoal in container with hole. Improves clarity and some chemicals. Does not sterilize. Must boil output water.
Layer gravel/sand/charcoal. Clarifies only. Output must be boiled before drinking.
Wait 30 minutes after last audible thunder. Storms can regenerate. Strikes occur up to 15km from cloud edge. Resume travel only after full wait.
Wait 30min after last thunder. Strikes hit 15km from cloud. Storms regenerate. Wait full time.

Solar Noon: When sun reaches highest point (zenith), shadows align North-South. In Northern Hemisphere, shadow points North. In Southern Hemisphere, shadow points South. Occurs at local apparent noon, not necessarily 12:00 clock time.
Solar noon: Sun at highest point. Shadows point North (N hem) or South (S hem). True meridian line when shadow shortest.

Results: Power banks often 5V/2.1A or 2.4A, not true 3A. Voltage sags to 4.5V at 2.5A load. Some have auto-shutoff at low current (Pi idle <100mA triggers off). Also cable quality poor on power banks. Needs 5V/3A PD power bank.

Power bank underspecified:
1. Verify bank rated 5V/3A with PD (Power Delivery)
2. Many banks are 2.4A max; insufficient for Pi 5 peaks
3. Use short 20AWG cable; bank cables often 28AWG
Use hard bamboo for plough stick. Split softer bamboo for base with groove. Alternatively use fire saw method with two bamboo halves.
Use bamboo sections. 1. Hard stalk for rubbing stick. 2. Split softer piece for base. 3. Rub along groove until dust ignites.
Abdominal thrusts: Stand behind. Thumb side of fist above navel. Grasp with other hand. Quick upward pull into diaphragm. Repeat 5 times.

Abdominal thrusts:
1. Fist above navel
2. Other hand grips
3. Upward pull
4. Repeat 5 times
Connection: Set Repeater-2 to EU_868. Match mesh channel configuration including PSK. Use Long_Fast modem preset. Save settings. Reboot. Verify in node list.

Connect Repeater-2:
1. Set EU_868
2. Match channel config
3. Input PSK
4. Use Long_Fast
5. Reboot
Red Cross protocol: Check scene. Get consent. 5 back blows with heel of hand. 5 abdominal thrusts. Continue until clear. Call 911 if continues.

Red Cross method:
1. 5 back blows
2. 5 abdominal thrusts
3. Continue cycles
4. Call emergency
Fire plough: Cut groove in softwood base. Rub hardwood shaft rapidly along groove with downward pressure. Friction creates hot dust that ignites in the groove.
Rub hard on soft. 1. Cut groove in softwood hearth. 2. Rub hardwood shaft along groove fast. 3. Push hot dust onto tinder when smoking.
Emergency transmission complete. Relayed by Node C.
SOS transmitted. Relayed via Node C.
Unit-4 invisible: Check EU_868 region set. Verify channel PSK identical to mesh. Confirm modem preset matches. Check power and antenna connections. May be out of range.

Debug Unit-4:
1. Check EU_868
2. Verify PSK
3. Confirm preset
4. Check connections
5. Check range
Place sealed metal container on exhaust manifold or radiator. Takes 20-30 min. Risk of fuel contamination. Check for leaks before drinking.
Seal container on exhaust manifold 20-30 min. Check for fuel contamination.

High drain cells (20A+) typically achieve 300-400 cycles to 80% capacity at 10A discharge. High current accelerates anode degradation. Stay below 5A for 1000+ cycles. Heat is primary degradation factor.
High drain (10A): 300-400 cycles. Stay <5A for 1000+. Heat kills cells.
Emergency relayed
Fire SOS relayed.

Massive hemorrhage arm protocol: Tourniquet immediately if severe. Place high and tight on upper arm if injury location unknown. Otherwise 5 cm proximal to wound. Direct pressure while preparing tourniquet. Elevate. Treat shock. Keep patient lying down.

Address massive hemorrhage:
1. Tourniquet immediately high and tight on upper arm
2. Apply direct pressure while preparing device
3. Elevate arm and treat for shock immediately
Log debris hut: Lean branches against log creating roof slope. Cover with leaves grass 1ft thick. Log acts as back wall and insulation. Entrance on open side.

Improve log shelter:
1. Lean poles on log side
2. Thatch with grass 1ft
3. Block ends with debris
4. Bed between ground and log
Make SOS 3m using rocks/cloth. Contrast is key. Clear 10m. Visible 4km.
Lemon and copper/zinc nails can create battery but voltage too low for steel wool ignition. Not practical for fire starting despite myths.
Not practical. 1. Lemon cell creates only 0.7 volts. 2. Insufficient to heat wool. 3. Better to use friction methods instead.
Checklist: 1) Shelter basement/center room. 2) Seal openings tape/plastic. 3) KI 130 mg <40 years old. 4) Stay 48-100 hours. 5) Avoid contaminated food/water. 6) Decontaminate clothing outside.

Emergency checklist:
1. Basement/center room shelter
2. Seal openings tape/plastic
3. 130 mg KI if <40 years
4. Stay 48-100 hours
Connection method: Hold button 5s. Match 868.7 MHz, preset SHORT_SLOW. Encryption key must be identical.
Hold 5s to connect. Match 868.7 MHz and SHORT_SLOW. Keys must be identical.
SteriPen type devices kill viruses/bacteria in 90 seconds. Requires clear water and charged batteries. Not effective on chemical contamination.
UV pen 90 sec for clear water. Needs charged batteries. No chemical removal.

Summit lightning protocol: Descend immediately. 30/30 rule applies. Avoid solitary trees by 20 meters. Seek lower ground or depression. Wait thirty minutes after final thunder before regaining elevation.

Get down now:
1. Descend from summit immediately
2. Maintain 20m from any tree
3. Find a low spot or depression
4. Wait 30 min after last thunder

Discharge permitted to -20°C with significant capacity loss. Power output drops due to high impedance. Below -20°C risk of copper dissolution from anode, causing internal shorts when warmed. Avoid heavy loads below 0°C.
Discharge to -20°C only. Power drops. Below -20°C = copper dissolution.
Dry stones in ring. Layer flat rocks. Prevents steam from wet soil. Insulates fire bed. Use if wood scarce. Can crack if wet stones heated rapidly.

Dry stones only:
1. Flat rock layer
2. Prevents steam
3. Insulates bed
4. Avoid wet rocks

Lightning position: Squat low with feet together, heels touching. Do not lie flat. Minimize ground contact. Cover ears. If in group, spread 15m apart. Avoid being tallest object. Wait 30 minutes after last thunder.

Assume safe position:
1. Squat, feet together
2. Do not lie down
3. Cover ears
4. Stay 15m from others
EMERGENCY broadcast from node 8. Medical priority.
Emergency from node 8. Medical. Copy?
Sassafras (Sassafras albidum). Root bark tea traditional. Safrole compound carcinogenic. FDA restricted. Limit use.
Sassafras. Root tea. Carcinogenic safrole. Limit.
Gable: Ridgeline center poles at ends creating A-shape. Ends closed with tarp triangles or gear. 7ft long accommodates 2 people sitting. Stake sides every 3ft.

Build gable:
1. Ridgeline center
2. Poles at ends
3. Close ends with gear
4. Stake sides tight
Rubber burns black. Leaves burn white. 3 puffs signal distress. Range 8km.
DANGER warning from node 3. Wildlife threat.
Danger from node 3. Wildlife threat.
Typical 18650 cells provide 300-500 charge cycles before capacity drops to 80%. High-drain cells may achieve 700-1000 cycles under optimal charging conditions (0.5C rate, 25°C ambient).
300-500 cycles typical. Retire at 80% capacity.
Basement or center of middle floor. Shielding: 20cm concrete reduces dose by 50%. Seal vents with plastic. Duration: 3 days optimal for dose reduction.

Location priority:
1. Basement first, then center floor
2. 20cm concrete halves exposure
3. Seal vents with plastic/tape
4. Remain 72h for dose reduction
Ostrich Fern (Matteuccia struthiopteris). Edible cooked. Must boil 10 min minimum. Raw causes illness.
Ostrich Fern. Safe cooked 10 min. Never raw.
NVMe peaks 2A, causes 0.6V drop in 26AWG cable. Fix: 5.1V/5A supply, 18AWG 0.5m, or NVMe HAT with external power input, use short quality cable.
NVMe peaks 2A drop 0.6V. Fix: 1. 5.1V/5A 2. 18AWG 0.5m 3. HAT external power

Profuse arm bleeding protocol: Apply direct pressure with hemostatic agent if available. Elevate limb. If bleeding continues, apply tourniquet 2-3 inches above wound on the arm. Twist rod until bleeding stops and secure. Write time on forehead or tourniquet.

Halt profuse bleeding now:
1. Press hard with hemostatic agent or clean cloth
2. Raise arm high above the heart
3. Apply and twist tourniquet 2-3 inches above injury
Broadcast complete. All nodes returning to base.
Continue until: AED arrives and ready, victim shows signs of life, rescuers exhausted, or professional help takes over. Can be 30+ minutes.

Until:
1. AED ready to use
2. Victim breathes
3. Help arrives
4. You collapse
5. 30+ minutes typical
Saguaro and barrel cactus liquid safe in emergency but acidic. Filter out pulp. Small amounts only. Boil not needed if fresh cut and healthy.
Barrel cactus: safe acidic liquid. Filter pulp. Small amounts. Fresh cut ok.
Error indicates input <4.63V. Common with 5V3A supplies under transient load. Solution: 27W PD supply, short heavy-gauge cable (20AWG), disable WiFi/BT if not needed to reduce draw.
Input <4.63V detected. Fix: 1. 27W PD supply 2. 20AWG short cable 3. Disable unused radios
First aid: 1) Lay flat 2) Raise legs 3) Keep warm 4) Monitor breathing 5) Call for help if not alone.

First aid:
1. Lay flat
2. Raise legs
3. Keep warm
4. Monitor breath

Peeling wrapper exposes metal can. Swelling indicates internal pressure. Combination creates short circuit risk between positive terminal and can side. Isolate immediately. Do not use tape to rewrap swollen cells.
Peel + swell = short risk. Do not rewrap. Isolate immediately.

Static electricity means strike imminent within seconds. Do not lie down. Immediately squat on balls of feet, heels touching, cover ears. Move away from metal. If in group, spread out. Descent may be too late.

Strike imminent:
1. Squat immediately
2. Heels together, cover ears
3. Move from metal

Risk of burn and short circuit. Use insulated gloves if possible. Keep face away from flash. Ensure no flammable liquids nearby. Dispose of depleted battery properly.

Victims do not retain charge. Safe to touch. Check breathing and pulse. CPR if needed. Treat burns. Evacuate if heart rhythm abnormal. Multiple casualties possible.
Safe to touch. Check breathing/CPR. Treat burns. Victims not charged. Evacuate if heart abnormal.
Emergency SOS sent.
NODE-B heartbeat received. RSSI -105, SNR 6.
Node B heard. Signal moderate. OK.
25W panel required for 5W load with 3 day autonomy. Use 60Wh 3S2P 18650 pack with CN3722 MPPT module. 12V nominal system.

Size for 25 W PV and 60 Wh pack:
1. 25 W panel (cloudy)
2. 3S2P 18650 cells
3. CN3722 MPPT module
4. 3 days autonomy
Connection steps: Power device. Set region EU_868 via app. Configure primary channel with mesh PSK. Select Long_Fast modem preset. Reboot. Device joins automatically.

Connect device:
1. Power on device
2. Set EU_868 region
3. Enter mesh PSK
4. Select Long_Fast preset
5. Reboot to join
Tinder: ignites with spark (grass, birch bark). Kindling: pencil to thumb size sticks that catch from tinder. Fuel: larger logs that sustain fire.
Three stages. 1. Tinder: catches spark easily. 2. Kindling: small sticks to build flame. 3. Fuel: large wood for sustained heat.
Visibility: Size 3m+, contrast, shadows (build up edges), location in open, not under canopy.

Signal visibility:
1. Build 3D edges for shadow
2. High contrast colors
3. Open area no trees
4. Large as possible
5. Align east-west for sun
Gateway-1 invisible: Region mismatch (need EU_868). Wrong channel PSK. Different modem preset. Gateway may be offline. Check power and antenna. Verify not in standalone mode.

Locate Gateway-1:
1. Verify EU_868
2. Check PSK matches
3. Confirm modem preset
4. Check power status
5. Exit standalone mode
Trail marker reported.
Water boils at lower temps at altitude (90°C at 3000m), so extend time to 3 minutes. Chemical treatments work normally. SODIS works but may need longer exposure due to thinner atmosphere UV.

Altitude adjustments:
1. Boil 3 minutes (lower boiling point)
2. Chemicals work normally
3. SODIS may need longer
4. Pressure affects boil temp
Step 1: Hold button 6s. Step 2: Set 868.9 MHz. Step 3: Choose MEDIUM_SLOW. Step 4: Match keys.
1. Hold button 6s. 2. Set 868.9 MHz. 3. Choose MEDIUM_SLOW. 4. Match keys exactly.
Rescue maneuvers: Back blows using heel of hand between shoulders. Abdominal thrusts with fist above navel pulling upward. Alternate 5 each until clear.

Rescue moves:
1. Back blows x5
2. Thrusts x5
3. Alternate
4. Call 911 if needed
Start CPR immediately. Compress 5-6cm deep at 100-120/min. 30:2 ratio. Check pulse every 2 minutes if trained. Continue until help arrives.

Start now:
1. Check pulse 10 sec
2. Push 5-6cm deep
3. 100-120 rate
4. 30:2 cycles
5. Recheck every 2 min
Emergency ground sign: Three elements S-O-S, each 3 meters. Use available debris with opposite tone to background. Visibility 5km.
Mark SOS with logs 3m long. High contrast only. Open ground. Seen 5km.
Smoke signals: Green vegetation produces white smoke; rubber/oil produces black. 3 puffs = distress. Single = location.
Puff code: 3 = distress. White = green leaves. Black = rubber. Range 5-10km.

Management of severe forearm lacerations: Apply direct pressure with bulky dressing. Elevate arm overhead if possible. For uncontrolled bleeding, apply tourniquet 5-7 cm above the laceration on the upper arm. Ensure tourniquet is tight enough to occlude arterial flow. Monitor for shock.

Apply pressure to laceration:
1. Pack wound with bulky dressing and press hard
2. Hold arm elevated overhead
3. Tighten tourniquet on upper arm if blood keeps gushing
Relayed to Alpha: Cave found.

Radial/ulnar artery injury management: Immediate digital pressure over vessel proximal to wound. Apply pressure bandage. Elevate hand above head. If unsuccessful, apply tourniquet to forearm 5 cm proximal to wrist. Tighten until spurting stops. Immobilize arm.

Control arterial spurting:
1. Apply digital pressure proximal to wrist wound
2. Elevate hand well above head level
3. Apply forearm tourniquet 5 cm above wrist if needed
Treating: Lay flat immediately, elevate legs 30-45 degrees, space blanket, check airway, monitor every 3 minutes.

Treating:
1. Flat now
2. Legs 45deg
3. Space blanket
4. Check airway
Immediate: Lay horizontal, legs elevated 30cm, thermal protection, pulse monitoring. Signs include pallor and confusion.

Shock emergency:
1. Lay down flat
2. Legs up
3. Keep warm
4. Monitor vitals

SODIS is the only option without fire or chemicals. Requires clear PET bottle and sunlight. 6 hours exposure. Alternatively, let sediment settle, then decant carefully, but this does not disinfect fully.

Only SODIS remains:
1. Clear PET bottle required
2. Fill, cap tightly
3. Lay in direct sun 6+ hrs
4. UV-A radiation disinfects
Settling alone is insufficient

For traumatic lacerations with heavy bleeding: 1) Apply firm direct pressure with hemostatic gauze or clean fabric. 2) Raise the extremity above heart level. 3) If bleeding is arterial (spurting), apply a tourniquet 2-3 inches above the wound between the heart and injury. Do not cover the tourniquet with clothing.

Control bleeding immediately:
1. Apply firm pressure with hemostatic gauze or cloth
2. Raise arm above heart level
3. Place tourniquet 2-3 inches above wound if spurting continues
Copper has oligodynamic effect killing some bacteria after 16 hours standing. Not reliable for viruses or immediate use. Boiling still required.
Copper kills some bacteria in 16 hrs. Unreliable. Boil regardless.
Bough bed: Cut spruce tips 2ft long. Lay criss-cross layers 6 inches thick creates mattress off cold ground. Add 1ft thick on sides for wind block if no shelter.

Build bough bed:
1. Cut tips 2ft long
2. Lay cross-hatch 6in
3. Add side windbreaks
4. Sleep in center depression
Fast method: Press button once, wait 5s, press again. Use 868.0 MHz, MEDIUM_FAST, channel hash 0xAB.
Press once, wait 5s, press again. Use 868.0 MHz and MEDIUM_FAST. Hash 0xAB required.
Polish aluminum can bottom with chocolate to create parabolic mirror. Focus sunlight onto tinder. Requires bright sun and patience.
Make solar reflector. 1. Polish can bottom with chocolate bar. 2. Wipe clean to create mirror. 3. Focus sun onto tinder for ignition.
Plastic sheeting barriers, wet cloth filters, tape seals. Reduce ventilation. Shelter time 24-48 hours. Highest protection factor location in building.

Modify building fast:
1. Plastic barriers on windows
2. Wet cloth over vents
3. Tape all perimeter seals
4. Maintain 24-48h isolation
Signs: victim gasps, color improves, pulse returns. Continue until definitive help arrives. Push 5-6cm at 100-120 bpm with 30:2 ratio.

Check for:
1. Gasping
2. Color return
3. Pulse check
4. Continue 5-6cm
5. 100-120 bpm
Length: 8-10 inches. Diameter: thumb to pinky finger. Straight and round. Hardwood like hickory, osage, or cedar. Dry and seasoned.
Choose straight dry wood. 1. Length: 20-25 cm. 2. Diameter: thumb size. 3. Hardwood: hickory or cedar preferred.
Immediate exit: >1 mSv/h. Urgent evacuation: 100-1000 µSv/h. Monitoring: <100 µSv/h. Annual limit 1 mSv, emergency limit 50 mSv. Time-distance-shielding protocol.

Exit triggers:
1. >1 mSv/h immediate departure
2. 100-1000 µSv/h urgent move
3. <100 µSv/h monitor
4. Max 50 mSv emergency dose
CPU: 42%, RAM: 58%, Temp: 42°C, Battery: 16%, LoRa RSSI: -109dBm, SNR: -4dB
CPU 42°C, Bat 16%, Radio -109dBm SNR -4dB weak.
Continuous warning from voltage <4.55V. Critical: cable resistance too high or PSU voltage low. Use 5.1V/5A supply, 18AWG cable, check GPIO power draw if used.
Voltage <4.55V detected. Fix: 1. 5.1V/5A supply 2. 18AWG cable 3. Check GPIO power

Prions and some bacterial spores (like Clostridium) can survive brief boiling, but these are rare in water. Standard 1-minute boil kills all common waterborne pathogens (cholera, typhoid, E. coli, viruses).

Rare exceptions exist:
1. Prions survive boiling
2. Some bacterial spores resist
3. Not found in typical water
4. 1 min boil kills cholera/E.coli
Standard boil is sufficient

Remaining cycles depend on depth of discharge and charge rate. Shallow cycles (30-80%) yield 2000+ cycles. Full cycles (0-100%) reduce to 500. Measure internal resistance increase to gauge remaining life.
Shallow cycles: 2000+. Deep cycles: 500. Check internal resistance.
SOS dimensions: Each letter 3x3 meters. Stroke width 1 meter. Spacing 1 meter between letters. Total 9-11 meters long.

SOS dimensions:
1. Each letter 3m high
2. Stroke width 1m
3. Gap between letters 1m
4. Total length 9m
5. Proportion 3:3:3
Self-choking: Make fist above navel. Grasp with other hand. Pull upward sharply against firm surface such as chair back or railing. Repeat until object dislodged.

Self rescue:
1. Fist above navel
2. Thrust against chair
3. Repeat until clear
4. Call 911 if possible
Pregnant or obese: Use chest thrusts instead of abdominal. Place fist on center of sternum. Pull straight back. Repeat 5 times. Alternate with back blows.

Pregnant/obese:
1. Use chest thrusts
2. Fist on sternum
3. Pull straight back
4. Alternate with back blows
Clear PET plastic bottles are standard (1-2 liter). Glass blocks UV-B needed for disinfection. PVC can leach chemicals. Bottles must be clean and unscratched for UV penetration.

Container rules:
1. Clear PET plastic only
2. 1-2 liter bottles best
3. Glass blocks UV-B
4. No PVC (chemical leaching)
Clean and unscratched
First response: Determine if partial or complete. If complete blockage begin 5 back blows immediately. Then 5 thrusts. Alternate until resolved.

First response:
1. Check if complete
2. 5 back blows
3. 5 thrusts
4. Repeat
Infant under 1 year: Place face down on forearm. 5 back slaps between shoulders. Turn over. 5 chest thrusts with two fingers on sternum. Repeat until object out.

Infant under 1yr:
1. 5 back slaps
2. Turn over
3. 5 chest thrusts
4. Repeat until clear
Add device: Hold 3s, release, hold 3s. Match 868.8 MHz, SHORT_SLOW preset. Range limit 9km for stable pairing.
Hold 3s, release, hold 3s. Match 868.8 MHz and SHORT_SLOW. Max range 9km.
Persistent warning indicates <4.6V continuously. Check: PSU rating (min 27W), cable AWG (min 22), length (max 0.8m). Solution: Official supply, heavy gauge short cable.
Continuous <4.6V. Fix: 1. Min 27W PSU 2. Min 22AWG 3. Max 0.8m length
Paper Birch (Betula papyrifera). Sap edible, water-like, early spring only. Inner bark edible emergency food.
Paper Birch. Sap drinkable spring. Inner bark edible dried.
Quick link: Triple-press. Match 868.2 MHz, LONG_FAST preset. Both units must be within 2km for initial pairing.
Triple-press to link. Match 868.2 MHz and LONG_FAST. Stay within 2km initially.
Ground SOS: Letters 3m, high contrast. Rocks on sand or snow. Visibility 6km.
Signal SOS: 3m letters, clear area 10m. Contrast colors. Detected 4-6km.

Rocky outcrops conduct electricity. Wet rock is especially dangerous. Move off immediately. Descend 50+ meters if possible. Avoid caves with dripping water. Maintain 20m from cliff edges. 30/30 rule applies.

Leave rocks now:
1. Wet rock conducts
2. Move 50m+ down
3. Avoid wet caves
4. Stay 20m from edges

Results: SSDs spike 1-2A on spin-up/read. USB 3.0 port provides 1.2A max. If SSD draws 2A, total Pi load exceeds 3A PSU capacity, causing 5V rail droop. Use SSD with external power or powered USB hub.

SSD current spike exceeding budget:
1. Use powered USB hub for SSD
2. Get SSD enclosure with external power input
3. Check SSD specs: some peak 2.5A on startup
CPU: 77%, RAM: 22%, Temp: 77°C, Battery: 5%, LoRa RSSI: -125dBm, SNR: -12dB
CPU 77°C hot, RAM 22%, Bat 5% critical, RSSI -125dBm.
Upgrade from 5V3A to 5.1V/5A or 27W PD. Use 18AWG 0.5m cable. Current setup likely has 0.4V drop across thin/long cable causing <4.6V at Pi.
Upgrade to 5.1V/5A or 27W PD. Use: 1. 18AWG cable 2. Max 0.5m 3. Check voltage drop
Fill clear bag with water and twist to form sphere or lens shape. Focus sunlight onto tinder. Works like magnifying glass.
Create water lens. 1. Fill clear bag with water. 2. Twist to form convex shape. 3. Focus bright sun onto char cloth.
Medical evac: X symbol on ground. Mirror flashes. Orange panel international distress. Wave arms overhead.

Medical evac signal:
1. Large X on ground
2. Mirror flashes 3×
3. Orange panel if available
4. Arms crossed overhead
5. Stay near X marker
Lowbush Blueberry (Vaccinium angustifolium). Blue-black ripe fruit safe. White/pink flowers. Green unripe safe but sour.
Blueberry. Safe when blue. Green unripe sour.

Results: NVMe SSD peaks 2A during initialization. Combined with Pi 5 (1.5A), total 3.5A exceeds 3A PSU. Voltage droops under peak load. Solution: powered USB hub for NVMe, or 5V/4A PSU, or limit SSD power management.

NVMe exceeding power budget:
1. Use PSU rated 5V/4A for Pi 5 + NVMe
2. Enable SSD power management: sudo sdparm --set=STANDBY
3. Use powered USB enclosure instead of HAT
Sustained undervoltage from cable resistance >0.5ohm or PSU voltage <4.9V. Fix: 5.1V regulated supply, 18AWG 0.8m cable, ensure USB-C contacts clean and tight.
Cable resistance >0.5ohm. Fix: 1. 5.1V regulated PSU 2. 18AWG 0.8m cable 3. Clean contacts
Split wood exposes dry interior. Use feather sticks. Find birch bark or resin. Build platform off wet ground. Use small dry twigs from lower branches of standing dead trees.

Split to dry core:
1. Feather sticks
2. Birch bark/resin
3. Platform off ground
4. Dead standing twigs
Boiling: rolling boil 1 min (3 min above 2000m). Bleach: 2 drops per liter of 5-6% solution, wait 30 min. SODIS: 6 hours full sun in clear PET bottle. Pre-filter turbid water through cloth.

Boil if you can; alternatives:
1. Rolling boil 1 min (3 min >2000 m)
2. Bleach: 2 drops/L, wait 30 min
3. SODIS: 6 hrs sun in clear bottle

Profuse arm bleeding protocol (PABC): Pressure first with sterile dressing. Apply firm continuous pressure. Assess circulation. If fails, tourniquet 5-7 cm proximal. Bandage securely. Check distal pulse after tourniquet. Monitor vital signs. Treat for shock.

Follow profuse bleeding protocol:
1. Apply continuous firm pressure with sterile dressing
2. Assess circulation and elevate limb
3. Tourniquet 5-7 cm proximal if pressure insufficient
Isolated: 30:2 ratio, 5-6cm depth, 100-120 bpm. Extended duration. Rest only when absolutely necessary. Continue until evacuation possible.

Isolated survival:
1. 30:2 ratio
2. 5-6cm deep
3. 100-120 bpm
4. Extended duration
5. Until evacuation

Tourniquet criteria for arm lacerations: Use when direct pressure fails to control bleeding after 3-5 minutes, for arterial spurting, or amputations. Place 5-7 cm proximal to wound on upper arm or forearm. Never over joints. Tighten until bleeding stops. Time application.

Apply tourniquet for laceration:
1. Place 5-7 cm proximal to wound on upper arm
2. Tighten until bleeding completely stops
3. Note time and do not loosen until definitive care
MAYDAY received from node 22. Requesting extraction.
Mayday node 22. Extraction request. Urgent.
High risk from animal feces despite crystal appearance. Boil 3 min at altitude or use 1 micron absolute filter. Chemicals less effective on cysts.
Clear but risky from animals. Boil 3 min altitude. Or 1 micron filter.
Sent. Medevac helicopter dispatched to coordinates.
Sent. Medevac dispatched to coordinates.
Standard symbols: SOS (distress), X (medical), V (assistance needed), Y (yes/ok), N (no). Minimum 10ft/3m.

Pilot symbols:
1. SOS = distress
2. X = need medical
3. V = need help
4. Y = yes/affirmative
5. Make 3 meters wide minimum
Clammy skin indicates shock. Aid: Immediate supine position, leg elevation 45 degrees, warmth, reassure casualty, check breathing.

Clammy skin:
1. Supine now
2. Legs 45deg
3. Warmth
4. Reassure
Heimlich: Place thumb side of fist slightly above navel. Grasp with other hand. Pull inward and upward with quick thrust. Repeat until obstruction clears or victim becomes unconscious.

Heimlich steps:
1. Fist above belly button
2. Grab with other hand
3. Pull up sharply
4. Repeat 5 times
30W panel minimum for winter operation of Pi 5. Pair with 50Wh LiFePO4 4S pack for better cold performance. MPPT essential for low irradiance.

Use 30 W panel with LiFePO4:
1. 30 W min winter
2. 4S LiFePO4 pack (50 Wh)
3. MPPT for low light
4. Cold tolerant chemistry
CPU: 55%, RAM: 48%, Temp: 55°C, Battery: 70%, LoRa RSSI: -76dBm, SNR: 4.2dB
Temp 55°C, Fuel 70%, RSSI -76dBm SNR 4dB.

Severed vein management (arm): Veins ooze rather than spurt but can cause significant blood loss. Apply direct pressure with bulky dressing. Elevate above heart. Pressure bandage wrapped snugly. Tourniquet rarely needed for veins unless massive injury. Monitor circulation.

Control severed vein bleeding:
1. Apply bulky dressing with direct pressure
2. Elevate arm well above heart level
3. Wrap pressure bandage snugly; tourniquet rarely needed
Marker SOS: 3m tall, opposite colors. Clear area. Range 5km.
SOS: 3m symbols, high contrast. Open terrain. Visible to aircraft 5km.
Field aid: Recognize pallor, sweating, weakness. Position casualty flat, elevate legs on pack, use space blanket, monitor pulse.

Field aid:
1. Flat ground
2. Pack under legs
3. Space blanket
4. Pulse check
Super shelter: Lean-to with mylar backing clear plastic front. Fire 5ft from opening. Reflector wall behind fire. Plastic traps heat mylar reflects. Can raise temp 40°F.

Build super shelter:
1. Lean-to with mylar back
2. Plastic sheet front
3. Reflector wall
4. Fire 5ft from opening
Sent. Base acknowledged. Resupply dispatched.
Sent to Base. Resupply dispatched.
Base-Node missing: Verify EU_868 region. Check channel PSK matches mesh exactly. Confirm modem preset. Check if Base-Node powered and antenna connected. Check for hardware failure.

Debug Base-Node:
1. Verify EU_868
2. Check PSK match
3. Confirm preset
4. Check power/antenna
5. Check hardware
Shutdown occurs <4.0V. Warning starts <4.63V. Prevention: 5.1V/5A supply, 18AWG 0.5m cable, no GPIO power, powered USB hub for all peripherals.
Warning <4.63V shutdown <4.0V. Fix: 1. 5.1V/5A supply 2. 18AWG 0.5m 3. Powered hub
Recovery: Double-press button. Check 868.8 MHz, LONG_FAST preset. Re-establish link within 3km.
Double-press to recover. Check 868.8 MHz and LONG_FAST. Stay within 3km.
Greater Burdock (Arctium lappa). Root edible. Leaves edible young. Burrs stick to clothing. Detoxifying.
Burdock. Root edible. Peel and boil. Young leaves ok.
FBAO treatment: Stand behind victim. 5 sharp back blows. 5 abdominal thrusts. Continue until object expelled or medical help arrives. Do not delay.

Object in airway:
1. 5 back slaps
2. 5 belly thrusts
3. Keep alternating
4. Call emergency
Sharp scraper at 45 degrees. Push rod away from you. Sparks fly downward. Scrape quickly for heat. Shower of molten metal. Aim directly into tinder center.

45 degree scrape:
1. Sharp spine
2. Quick downward pull
3. Aim at center
4. Shower of sparks
Stay minimum 10m from isolated trees. Lightning strikes tallest object. In forest, stay among shorter trees. Spread group 20m apart to prevent multiple casualties.
Stay 10m from isolated trees. Spread group 20m apart. Find shorter trees in forest.
Flee if >1 mSv/h and route safe. Shelter if route unsafe or <100 µSv/h. Take KI 130 mg if radioiodine suspected and <40 years old. All within first hour of warning.

Decision tree:
1. >1 mSv/h + safe route = flee
2. Unsafe route or <100 µSv/h = shelter
3. <40y + radioiodine = 130 mg KI
4. Execute within 1 hour
Warning broadcast. 6 nodes acknowledged receipt.
Storm warning sent. 6 nodes received.
Place hands flat on spindle top. Press down while rubbing hands back and forth to rotate. Move hands down as spindle shortens.
Rotate with palms. 1. Place flat hands on stick. 2. Press down while rubbing. 3. Move down stick as it burns shorter.

Results: Unlikely direct cause, but RF dongles can spike 100-200mA during transmission. With marginal PSU (2.5A rated, drops at 2A), small additions trigger undervoltage. Root cause still PSU/cable, not the dongle.

Dongle tipping marginal supply:
1. Remove dongle: if warning clears, supply was borderline
2. Replace PSU with genuine 5V/3A
3. Use keyboard with receiver on powered hub

Open-sided shelters unsafe. Lightning travels through wiring and supports. No protection from side flash or ground current. Seek enclosed building or vehicle. If only option, crouch in center away from supports.

Open shelters unsafe:
1. Current travels supports
2. No side flash protection
3. Seek enclosed building
4. Or crouch center if trapped
Bleach is safer for long-term use. Iodine can cause thyroid issues with extended consumption. Both require 30 min contact time. Neither removes Cryptosporidium effectively; boiling does.

Bleach for long term:
1. Iodine harms thyroid over time
2. Bleach safer chronically
3. Both wait 30 min
4. Neither kills all cysts
Boil for cysts
Storm warning sent.
Cut high, drink low. Generally safe if vine healthy and not milky sap. Quantity limited. No purification needed if freshly cut.
Cut high drink low. Avoid milky sap. Fresh from healthy vine: safe. Limited qty.
60Wh minimum for 3 days (5W load). Use 3S2P 18650 or 4S LiFePO4 12Ah. Ensure 20W panel can recharge fully in one sunny day.

60 Wh minimum capacity:
1. 3S2P 18650 config
2. Or 4S LiFePO4 12Ah
3. 20 W panel recharges 1 day
4. DOD limited to 80%
CPU: 26%, RAM: 41%, Temp: 42°C, Battery: 95%, LoRa RSSI: -67dBm, SNR: 11dB
Temp 42°C, Battery 95%, RSSI -67dBm excellent.
Procedure: 1. Hold button 3s. 2. Set 868.2 MHz. 3. Choose SHORT_SLOW. 4. Match keys. 5. Wait 20s.
1. Hold 3s. 2. Set 868.2 MHz. 3. Choose SHORT_SLOW. 4. Match keys. 5. Wait 20s.

Critical arm bleeding tourniquet facts: Place 2-4 inches (5-7 cm) proximal to wound on the upper arm (never on forearm for forearm injuries - too distal). Width minimum 1.5 inches. Tighten until bleeding stops and distal pulse absent. Note time. Do not remove in field.

Apply tourniquet correctly:
1. Place 2-4 inches proximal on upper arm only
2. Tighten until bleeding stops and pulse absent
3. Note time and never remove in field
Immediate protocol: Supine position, elevate legs 12 inches, maintain warmth, loosen tight clothing, reassure casualty.

Protocol now:
1. Supine
2. Legs 12in up
3. Warmth
4. Loosen tight
CPU: 44%, RAM: 53%, Temp: 44°C, Battery: 61%, LoRa RSSI: -84dBm, SNR: 6dB
CPU 44°C, Bat 61%, Net -84dBm SNR 6dB.
White smoke use green wood. Black use rubber. 3 puffs = SOS. Visible 6km.
CPU: 82%, RAM: 77%, Temp: 82°C, Battery: 22%, LoRa RSSI: -122dBm, SNR: -10dB
CPU 82°C hot, RAM 77%, Bat 22%, RSSI -122dBm bad.

Critical forearm laceration aid: Control bleeding first. Direct pressure with sterile dressing. Elevate. If involving major vessels or uncontrolled, tourniquet 5-7 cm proximal on upper arm. Do not remove tourniquet. Cover with clean dressing. Monitor vital signs continuously.

Provide critical aid:
1. Apply sterile dressing with direct pressure
2. Elevate forearm immediately

Yes. Rolling boil (100°C) kills bacteria, viruses and protozoa. Duration: 1 minute at sea level, 3 minutes above 2000 meters. Does not remove chemical contaminants.

Yes, boiling works:

3. Boil 3 min above 2000 m
Note: does not remove chemicals
Double battery capacity, keep panel at 20W minimum. For 5W load: 40Wh battery, 20W panel. For 10W load: 80Wh battery, 30W panel. MPPT required.
